title: Burghers in Paris in the Middle Ages - Medieval Histories
highlights:
Burghers in Paris in the Middle Ages - Medieval Histories ## This important book about life in Paris in the Middle Ages is written in the best tradition of the Annales -School.  ...    This is the latest work by one of the greatest historians of the twentieth century. The Burghers of Paris in the Middle Ages tells the story of traders, shopkeepers, artisans, financiers, doctors, employees of the royal administration, lawyers, clerics – who, together with the king, made Paris the first city in Europe. From the pen of Jean Favier we get picturesque details and anecdotes, which punctuate the narrative of the daily life in a city, which had no parallel in France in the Middle Ages. The medieval history of the Paris comes alive here. The  ...    Jean Favier (2 April 1932 – 12 August 2014) was a French historian, who specialized in Medieval history. From 1975 to  ...
